data.narrative:
AQT is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Rainer Blatt, Thomas Monz, and Peter Zoller. The valuation analysis draws from a sector comp universe of 11 quantum computing peers: 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this engagement. The quantum computing sector contains only 7 directly comparable companies, but filtering for same-stage and adjacent-stage peers with similar valuation tiers yielded fewer than three datapoints—below the minimum threshold for robust triangulation. An all-sector median approach would introduce excessive methodological noise given the heterogeneity in quantum hardware approaches, development timelines, and commercial maturity across the comp set.

Given data constraints, this valuation cannot be supported by quantitative triangulation at this time. Establishing AQT's range would require either (1) access to detailed recent financials and funding terms from at least three narrowly-filtered peers, or (2) a shift to alternative methods such as DCF analysis anchored to management guidance, or cost-of-capital benchmarking against public quantum peers. The analyst recommends deferring to a subsequent round when either additional comparable transactions materialize in the quantum space or management provides sufficient operational and financial detail to support bottom-up modeling.
